artifact.name: BD_00_AUTHORITY_TRACEABILITY_REGISTER.json
artifact.title: Lock P0 traceability and burndown authority boundary

active_ticket.execution_decision: Do not implement; produce authority register and wait for decision.

preflight.latest_commit: e454055 feat: add release candidate route capture mode
preflight.dirty_worktree_decision: Treat as pre-existing parallel/user work. Do not modify, revert, stage or rely on these files for BD-00 acceptance.

accepted_execution_authority.role: Repo-local operative authority for implementation, phase order, preflight, forbidden changes, safety gates and proof language.
Moving baseline for current repo reality; must beat older docs and screenshots.
Primary True-UX task source only through the handoff authority.
Flow architecture and process-first target-surface support.
Route evolution and split policy support; cannot authorize route creation by itself.
Decision authority and route evolution governance support.
True-UX doctrine and derivation support; not direct implementation authority.
Current requested execution plan and ticket order, subordinate to True-UX authority.
Input burndown and dependency map, subordinate to the derived ticket structure and True-UX authority.
Method prompt used to derive ticket architecture and CTES discipline, not project code truth.

excluded_or_demoted_sources.source: main branch, uploaded source snapshots, old bundles and stale generated audits
excluded_or_demoted_sources.reason: Explicitly not target truth under True-UX handoff and current moving-baseline discipline.

excluded_or_demoted_sources.source: screenshots without route, viewport, timestamp, capture mode and source-state provenance
excluded_or_demoted_sources.reason: BD-01 states screenshot acceptance proof is not trustworthy until capture proof integrity is repaired.
Declared priority counts conflict with actual item records.
They were present before this execution slice and must not be silently treated as BD-00 output.

traceability_finding_register.title: Input burndown summary priority counts do not match actual item records
impact: Priority-based burn charts would be wrong if they use the summary instead of item rows.
P0 readiness can be over- or under-reported.
Downstream execution should use item-level truth until the source summary is corrected.
traceability_finding_register.recommended_resolution: Create or approve a corrected canonical burndown baseline before using counts for planning, commit scopes or executive reporting.

extracted_execution_order.detailed_description: Technical proof repair ticket; can proceed before broad process UX if framed as proof integrity, not feature work.

dg_01_decision_options.label: Recommended: strict traceability rebase
dg_01_decision_options.decision: Approve the authority boundary, demote stale screenshots/old bundles/reference-only materials, treat item rows as canonical over summary counts, and authorize a corrected canonical burndown baseline before or alongside BD-01.
dg_01_decision_options.why_bold: This removes legacy ambiguity instead of carrying it as a permanent compatibility caveat.

dg_01_decision_options.label: Conservative: proceed with limitation
dg_01_decision_options.decision: Approve execution order and continue to BD-01 while preserving the count mismatch as a known limitation.
dg_01_decision_options.why_not_recommended: Fast, but it keeps a known reporting inconsistency alive.

dg_01_decision_options.label: Hard reset: regenerate burndown first
dg_01_decision_options.decision: Stop ticket execution and regenerate P0_PROCESS_FIRST_UX_BURNDOWN.json from source inputs before BD-01.
dg_01_decision_options.why_not_recommended: Cleanest on paper, but likely slower than correcting the traceability baseline now.

recommendation.rationale: Use the current ticket structure as the execution spine, but aggressively clean the baseline: item-level truth beats stale summary counts, unproven screenshots are reference-only until BD-01, and older bundles/docs must not compete with True-UX authority. This is the best path to remove legacy ambiguity instead of masking it.

decision_effect: Authority boundary is accepted for this run.
Item-level ticket truth is canonical over stale burndown summary counts.
Stale screenshots, old bundles and unproven captures remain reference-only unless BD-01 proof eligibility marks them stronger.
Execution may continue in the specified ticket order using strict traceability rebase discipline.

commit_decision.reason: DG-01 was approved with APPROVE_DG01_STRICT_TRACEABILITY_REBASE, so this register should be force-added as canonical run proof instead of remaining an ignored local artifact.
